operating_state: Net sales increased 5% to $43.3 billion driven by broad-based growth across all segments, with organic sales growth of 5% reflecting underlying business momentum excluding foreign exchange impacts. Q4 delivered 3% growth with 4% higher pricing offset by 1% unfavorable foreign exchange and unchanged unit volume.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 520 basis points to 52.7% driven by manufacturing productivity savings, lower commodity costs, and higher pricing, partially offset by unfavorable foreign exchange impacts. Cost of products sold decreased reflecting improved operational efficiency while SG&A expenses increased driven by higher marketing investments and inflationary pressures on labor and transportation costs.
material_events: The company recorded a $1.3 billion non-cash impairment charge on Gillette indefinite-lived intangible assets due to higher discount rates, currency weakening, and restructuring impacts. Management announced a limited market portfolio restructuring primarily in Argentina and Nigeria on December 5, 2023, expecting significant after-tax charges over fiscal 2024-2025. P&G completed $6.7 billion in share repurchases and declared quarterly dividend of $0.9407 per share.
macro_exposures: The company faces heightened exposure to foreign currency devaluation in Enterprise Markets including Argentina and Nigeria, driving the announced restructuring program. Russia-Ukraine war continues impacting operations with Russia representing approximately 2% of consolidated sales following March 2022 portfolio reduction. OECD/G20 tax policy changes including global minimum tax expected to impact multinational operations alongside ongoing commodity price volatility.
forward_outlook: Management expects incremental restructuring charges over fiscal years 2024-2025, primarily consisting of foreign currency translation losses upon substantial liquidation of operations in affected markets. The company reaffirmed expectations for direct share repurchases in fiscal 2024 while maintaining focus on productivity improvements and cost savings.
uncertainty_profile: The Gillette intangible asset remains sensitive to discount rate changes, growth rate assumptions, and currency fluctuations, with potential for additional impairment under adverse scenarios. Ongoing legal proceedings span multiple jurisdictions while future adverse business or macroeconomic changes may trigger additional asset impairments and Russia-Ukraine war impacts remain unpredictable.
